<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center of the view, facing upward. The cell directly ahead is lava, so forward is not safe. The left cell is a wall (#) and the right cell is floor (.). Since the goal is not visible, I need to explore. Turning clockwise will make me face right, allowing me to move onto the safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing east. The cell directly ahead (east) is a wall (#). The left cell is lava (L), and the right cell is out of bounds. The only safe adjacent cell is above (north), which is floor (.). To move north, I need to turn counter-clockwise to face north.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is lava, the left cell is a wall, and the right cell is floor. Turning clockwise to face east will allow us to move forward onto a floor cell, as seen in the previous east-facing observation. So I will turn clockwise now.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>

Preciso voltar para a cĂ©lula anterior, que era um piso seguro. Para isso, vou girar 180 graus (duas rotaĂ§Ăµes no sentido horĂ¡rio) para ficar virado para sul, e entĂ£o mover para frente.

<thought>
No observation atual, a cĂ©lula Ă  frente (norte na visĂ£o, que corresponde a leste no mundo) Ă© uma parede. A cĂ©lula Ă  esquerda (oeste na visĂ£o, que corresponde a norte no mundo) Ă© um piso, assim como a cĂ©lula Ă  direita (leste na visĂ£o, que corresponde a sul no mundo). No entanto, para o sul, apĂ³s um passo hĂ¡ uma parede (coluna 6 na visĂ£o), enquanto para o norte hĂ¡ uma sequĂªncia de pisos (colunas 3, 2, 1). Para explorar em direĂ§Ă£o a essas Ă¡reas desconhecidas, o melhor Ă© girar para o norte (anti-horĂ¡rio) e depois avanĂ§ar.

ApĂ³s girar de volta para a orientaĂ§Ă£o original, percebo que a cĂ©lula Ă  frente (norte) Ă© um piso seguro (`.`). Posso avanĂ§ar nessa direĂ§Ă£o para explorar mais o mapa e encontrar o objetivo O. As cĂ©lulas ao redor sĂ£o lava Ă  esquerda e parede Ă  direita, entĂ£o o movimento para frente Ă© a melhor opĂ§Ă£o.

<thought>
The cell directly north is floor (.), and both left and right are blocked by lava or wall. I will continue moving north to explore further and potentially reach the goal.
</thought>

<thought>
The cell directly north is floor (.), and there is no immediate danger. I will continue moving north to explore further and hopefully find the goal.
</thought>

<thought>
The forward cell is floor, so moving north is safe. This will bring me closer to the wall row, but I need to explore to find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north, but the cell directly in front is a wall. The cells to my left (west) and right (east) are lava and wall respectively, so I cannot move in those directions. The only safe adjacent cell is the one behind me (south), which I came from. I need to turn around to face south in order to move back and explore other possible paths to the goal. I will rotate clockwise to face east first, then I can rotate again to face south in the next step.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing east after turning clockwise. The cell directly east is a floor cell ('.'), which is safe to occupy. Moving forward will allow me to continue exploring the map and potentially find a path to the goal.
</thought>

<thought>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (^ indicates forward direction is up). The cell directly ahead is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. I need to turn clockwise to face east, where there are free floor cells (.) to explore. I will turn clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is now facing south (after two clockwise turns from north). The cell directly ahead (south) is a floor cell (.), so it is safe to move forward. There is a clear path ahead with floor cells. I will move forward to explore further and hopefully find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom of the view with a wall to the west and lava to the east. The cell to the north is a safe floor, leading to a vertical corridor. To move north, the agent must face north. Based on the previous movement, the agent likely faces south. Turning clockwise once will face west (toward a wall) â€“ not ideal but safe, as we will turn again to face north on the next step. Turning anti-clockwise would face east into deadly lava, so clockwise is safer.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing east. The goal O is at the top row, column 4. The grid has safe cells at odd rows (1,3,5,7) and some safe cells at even rows at specific columns: row2 col0, row4 col2, row6 col2. A viable path involves moving west to column 2, then north through the safe column 2 stepping stones: (7,2) -> (6,2) -> (5,2) -> (4,2) -> (3,2), then west to (3,0), north to (2,0), north to (1,0), and east along row1 to the goal. The immediate step is to turn to face west. Since the agent faces east, turning anti-clockwise once will face north. That is a step towards west (after a second turn). Moving forward now would go into lava north, so we must not move. Turning anti-clockwise now is safe.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north, and the cell directly ahead (north) is a safe floor (`.`). Moving forward is safe and will allow progress up the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The current observation shows the agent facing north. The cell directly ahead is floor, but further north leads to a wall at row2. The cell to the right (east) is also floor, and the east path might offer new exploration opportunities. Since the forward path is a dead end, I will turn clockwise to face east and then consider moving east.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing east. The goal is to reach the O at the top of the grid. A viable path goes north from the current position (6,3) to (5,3), then further north to (4,3) and (3,3), then west to column1, then north again to row1 and row0, then east along the top row. The cell directly north (5,3) is floor and safe. To proceed, I need to turn to face north first.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is now facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is a floor cell ('.'), safe to step onto. I will move forward to progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north. Directly ahead is floor, but the corridor ahead is a dead end with a wall two cells north and lava to the east. The cell behind me (south) is safe because I just came from there, and it leads to unexplored areas. I should turn around to head south to explore more options.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>
